patches.summary: Hand-authored parent-ID traversal so sync walks ClickUp's hierarchical resources (teams → spaces → folders → lists → tasks; teams → docs; teams → channels) instead of failing on the first list endpoint that requires a parent ID.
patches.reason: The generator's default sync layer assumes global list endpoints (e.g. /users, /repos). ClickUp requires a parent ID for almost every list operation, so without parent traversal every hierarchical resource fails sync with `unknown sync resource` or HTTP 404. This is a generic hierarchical-API gap likely worth backporting upstream; in the meantime the traversal lives here.
patches.validated_outcome: Default `sync` populates 116 records across 8 resource types in the printer's three workspaces (3 teams, 1 user, 6 spaces, 0 folders, 9 lists, 86 tasks, 2 docs, 9 channels).

patches.summary: Distinguish recognized-envelope-with-empty-array from singular-response in `extractPageItems` so tenants with zero records of a resource type sync cleanly instead of crashing.
patches.reason: ClickUp returns `{"spaces": []}` for workspaces with no spaces. The generator's original `extractPageItems` returned `(nil, "", false)` for both "unrecognized response shape" and "recognized envelope with empty array," causing the singular upsert path to fail with `missing id for space`. Generic OpenAPI bug worth backporting upstream.

patches.summary: Top-level `docs` and `chat` commands with idiomatic verbs (search, get, pages, page, listing, send, react, reply, members, followers, messages, etc.) replacing the spec-derived `workspaces docs <verb>-public` and `workspaces chat get-channels` paths. Original verbs preserved as Cobra aliases for back-compat.
patches.reason: The generator names commands after API operationIds. ClickUp's v3 docs and chat operations carry the `-public` suffix and live deeply nested under `workspaces`, which is rough on humans and on agent prompts. The aliases give a flatter, more memorable surface without losing the original paths.

patches.summary: Fix `--since` so it sends the correct query parameter and value format per resource: `date_updated_gt` (Unix ms) for v2 task; `with_message_since` (Unix seconds) for v3 chat; nothing for resources with no documented since support.
patches.reason: Greptile P1 finding on PR #433. The original `determineSinceParam()` returned the v3-chat-only `with_message_since` for every resource, which v2 endpoints silently ignore — so `clickup-pp-cli sync --since 7d` did a full sync of tasks/spaces/folders/lists with no error or warning. Per-resource mapping prevents the silent no-op; resources without since support now skip the param entirely instead of sending the wrong key.

patches.summary: Fix sync pagination so v2 endpoints use integer ?page=N and v3 endpoints use cursor pagination, instead of one cursor-shaped default that silently truncates v2 lists with >100 records.
patches.reason: Greptile P1 finding on PR #433. ClickUp v2 (e.g. /v2/list/{id}/task) uses ?page=0,1,2 pagination starting at 0 and never returns a cursor field. The original `determinePaginationDefaults()` returned `cursorParam: cursor` universally; the loop's break condition `nextCursor == ''` always fired after the first v2 page (no cursor ever returned), so any list with more than 100 tasks was silently truncated. Per-resource pagination strategy synthesizes nextCursor from page-counter math when style is `page`, leaving cursor-style behavior unchanged for v3 doc/chat. Verified end-to-end via --dry-run: task sends `?page=0&limit=100`, channel sends `?limit=100&with_message_since=...` (no page).

patches.summary: Rewrite `workflow archive` to archive every default resource through the shared `syncResource` engine instead of a private team+user fetch loop. Supersedes the earlier workflow-archive-v2-paths and workflow-archive-envelope-unwrap patches, which fixed the now-deleted loop.
patches.reason: Greptile finding on PR #433. The hand-written archive loop (a) listed only `team` and `user` while the command advertised "all syncable resources", so `workflow archive` followed by `search` for tasks/spaces/docs returned nothing, and (b) paginated v2 list endpoints with a `?after=<id>` cursor ClickUp ignores — an account with exactly 100 workspaces never satisfied the `len(items) < 100` break and re-fetched page one forever. Delegating to `syncResource` makes `workflow archive` honor its contract (all 8 resources, hierarchical parent-first order) and reuses the per-resource pagination (v2 page-number vs v3 cursor) and envelope handling already verified for the main `sync` command. The two prior patches that this replaces — the `/v2/` path prefix fix and the `extractPageItems` envelope unwrap — are now subsumed because the bespoke loop they patched no longer exists; `syncResource` already does both correctly.

patches.summary: Make `--full` and `--latest-only` restart hierarchical resources from the head. `syncResource` now ignores the stored resume cursor whenever full or latestOnly is set, instead of relying on caller-side clearing loops that only zeroed top-level sync_state keys.
patches.reason: Greptile findings on PR #433. The hierarchical-sync-traversal patch keys resume cursors per (resource, parentID) as `resource:parentID`. Both the `--full` clearing loop and the `--latest-only` clearing loop in `newSyncCmd` only called SaveSyncState/GetSyncState on the bare `resource` key, never the per-parent keys. If a prior hierarchical sync was interrupted mid-resource, `--full` and `--latest-only` left those `resource:parentID` cursors intact and `syncResource` resumed affected lists from page N instead of page 0 — contradicting `--full`'s documented 'ignore previous checkpoint' and `--latest-only`'s 'refresh the head'. Fixed at the point of use: `syncResource` takes a `latestOnly` parameter and zeroes the cursor whenever `full || latestOnly`, covering every target — global and per-parent alike. The redundant and incomplete caller-side clearing loops are removed; `newWorkflowArchiveCmd` passes latestOnly=false since `workflow archive` has no such flag.

patches.summary: Only clear a target's resume cursor when the page loop exhausted the resource naturally — not when the --max-pages cap cut it short.
patches.reason: Greptile finding on PR #433. After each per-target page loop, `syncResource` unconditionally wrote `SaveSyncState(ckey, "", totalCount)`, clobbering the resume cursor saved after the last fetched page. That is correct for natural exhaustion (the next run re-scans from the head) but wrong on a `--max-pages` cap hit: a workspace with more records than maxPages × pageLimit would re-fetch the same first maxPages pages on every run, leaving deeper records permanently unreachable. Track whether the loop broke on the cap (`capHit`) and skip the end-of-target cursor clear in that case so the next run resumes where it left off.
